records.ety: [F. dsobir; pref. ds- (L. dis-) + obir. See Obey, and cf. Disobedient.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disobeyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disobeying.]

defs: Not to obey; to neglect or refuse to obey (a superior or his commands, the laws, etc.); to transgress the commands of (one in authority); to violate, as an order; as, refractory children disobey their parents; men disobey their Maker and the laws.
quotes: Not to disobey her lord's behest.

defs: To refuse or neglect to obey; to violate commands; to be disobedient.
quotes: He durst not know how to disobey.
